title: corynephorus canescens subsp. maritimus
intro: Corynephorus canescens subsp. maritimus, also known as coastal cotton grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial plant native to coastal areas of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its soft, white or pale yellow plumes that resemble cotton. The medicinal properties of coastal cotton grass lie in its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and relieving coughs and congestion. The plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and supporting immune function.
